source: All-In Podcast — 2025 predictions (bestie guest Gavin Baker)
summary: Big businesses that use AI thoughtfully will be the primary winners in the near term due to test-time compute advantages
summary_extended: Baker argues that the primary near-term winners from AI will be large enterprises rather than small businesses, driven specifically by the economics of test-time compute. His reasoning is that big businesses can afford to spend $1M or more to let an AI model 'think' for six weeks on their most critical strategic questions — a cost that is prohibitive for small firms. This creates a structural, compounding advantage for large enterprises that can deploy AI at scale on high-value problems, amplifying the existing inequality between large and small businesses. The investment implication is thematic rather than tied to a specific company: investors should favor large-cap enterprises that are thoughtful AI adopters, though Baker does not name specific beneficiary stocks in this thesis. No specific timeframe beyond 'near term' is given, and no numerical return targets are cited.
detail: Baker argues that large enterprises can afford to spend $1M+ to let AI think for 6 weeks on their most important business questions, creating a structural advantage over small businesses. This amplifies inequality between large and small firms in the near term.
confidence_evidence: Baker makes this argument explicitly and unprompted as part of his biggest business winner prediction
quote_fragment: if you're a big business and you can pay a million dollars to let an AI think for 6 weeks
